rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUNNESTRAND et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, By Defendant Gencorp Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 19, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant GenCorp. Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating any triable issue of material fact. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it was the intent of the parties to the Settlement Agreement and Release that such release would apply to the particular conduct of the defendant which caused the harm, i.e., the manufacture, supply and/or distribution of asbestos-containing products to which Mr. Runnestrand was exposed and who subsequently developed his asbestos-related illness. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
